---
title: "Cyber.org K-12"
subtitle: "K-12 Cybersecurity Learning Standards"
jurisdiction: "US"
framework-type: "Pedagogy"
organizing-units: 116
elements: 500
license: "CC BY-NC 4.0"
date: 2026-05-08
categories: [pedagogy, us]
---

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Steward | Cyber Innovation Center / Cyber.org |
| Canonical page | [K-12 Cybersecurity Learning Standards](https://cyber.org/standards) |
| Version | 1.0 |
| Released | 2021-09-09 |
| Source format | PDF |
| License | Creative Commons BY-NC 4.0 |
| `framework_summary` slug | `cyberorg-k12-v1.0` |

## Structure in cybed: terms

::: {.column-page}

```{=html}
<figure class="schema-diagram">
<button type="button" class="toggle-all" aria-pressed="false" aria-label="Toggle schema names for all elements"><span class="toggle-icon" aria-hidden="true">▸</span><span class="toggle-text">schema names</span></button>
<svg viewBox="0 0 620 320" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-labelledby="cyberorg-structure-title">
  <title id="cyberorg-structure-title">Cyber.org K-12 structural mapping</title>
  <defs><marker id="cyb-arrow" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8.5" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse"><path d="M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.55"/></marker></defs>
  <g stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.4" fill="none" opacity="0.55">
    <line x1="310" y1="58" x2="310" y2="100" marker-end="url(#cyb-arrow)"/>
    <line x1="220" y1="170" x2="160" y2="220" marker-end="url(#cyb-arrow)"/>
    <line x1="400" y1="170" x2="460" y2="220" marker-end="url(#cyb-arrow)" stroke-dasharray="5,4"/>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-node" aria-label="Framework">
    <rect x="230" y="20" width="160" height="40" rx="6" fill="rgba(14,165,233,0.10)" stroke="#0284C7" stroke-width="1.6"/>
    <text class="lp" x="310" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">Framework</text>
    <text class="lt" x="310" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">cyberorg:Framework</text>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-node" aria-label="Standard group">
    <rect x="150" y="100" width="320" height="70" rx="6" fill="rgba(14,165,233,0.16)" stroke="#0284C7" stroke-width="1.6"/>
    <text class="lp" x="310" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">Standard group</text>
    <text class="lp" x="310" y="151" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="10.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.65">(grade band × sub-concept cell)</text>
    <text class="lt" x="310" y="125" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">cyberorg:StandardGroup</text>
    <text class="lt" x="310" y="145" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="10.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">= cybed:OrganizingUnit</text>
    <text class="lt" x="310" y="161" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="10.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.55">(no cybed:Role)</text>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-node" aria-label="Standard">
    <rect x="80" y="220" width="160" height="60" rx="6" fill="rgba(14,165,233,0.10)" stroke="#0284C7" stroke-width="1.6"/>
    <text class="lp" x="160" y="255" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">Standard</text>
    <text class="lt" x="160" y="245" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">cyberorg:Standard</text>
    <text class="lt" x="160" y="263" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="10.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">= cybed:RoleElement</text>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-node" aria-label="Example">
    <rect x="380" y="220" width="160" height="60" rx="6" fill="rgba(14,165,233,0.10)" stroke="#0284C7" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/>
    <text class="lp" x="460" y="245" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">Example</text>
    <text class="lp" x="460" y="263" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="10.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">clarification statements</text>
    <text class="lt" x="460" y="245" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">cybed:Example</text>
    <text class="lt" x="460" y="263" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="10.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">clarification statements</text>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-edge-label" aria-label="Framework-to-standard-group connection">
    <text class="ep" x="318" y="84" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">contains</text>
    <text class="et" x="318" y="84" font-family="JetBrains Mono,monospace" font-size="10" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">cybed:hasOrganizingUnit</text>
  </g>
  <g class="cyb-edge-label" aria-label="Standard-group-to-standard connection">
    <text class="ep" x="195" y="200" text-anchor="end" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">has element</text>
    <text class="et" x="195" y="200" text-anchor="end" font-family="JetBrains Mono,monospace" font-size="10" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">cybed:hasElement</text>
  </g>
  <g class="cyb-edge-label" aria-label="Standard-group-to-example connection">
    <text class="ep" x="425" y="200" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11.5" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">has example</text>
    <text class="et" x="425" y="200" font-family="JetBrains Mono,monospace" font-size="10" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">cybed:hasExample</text>
  </g>
</svg>
<figcaption>Numbered standards attach to each standard group. Clarification statements are reached through a separate connection. Click any box or edge label to flip between plain English and the technical schema name.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

:::

## Counts

| Measure | Value |
|---|---|
| Standard groups | 116 |
| Strict elements (numbered standards) | 123 |
| With-examples elements | 500 |
| Examples (clarification statements) | 377 |
| Elements per standard group (strict) | 1.1 |
| Elements per standard group (with examples) | 4.3 |

The strict count reflects only numbered standards. The
with-examples count promotes each Clarification statement to a
queryable node, which roughly quadruples the apparent density.
Use the strict count when comparing against frameworks (NICE, DCWF,
SFIA) that encode all detail in numbered statements. Use the
with-examples count when the comparison is about what reaches a
classroom.

## Provenance

::: {.provenance-cards .column-page}

::: {.provenance-card}
<svg class="provenance-icon" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#icon-archive"/></svg>

**Source**

Cyber.org K-12 Learning Standards distribution.
:::

::: {.provenance-card}
<svg class="provenance-icon" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#icon-gear"/></svg>

**Ingestion**

`scripts/010-ingest-cyberorg.R` parses standards and clarification statements into the cybed schema.
:::

::: {.provenance-card}
<svg class="provenance-icon" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#icon-license"/></svg>

**License**

Creative Commons BY-NC 4.0, non-commercial use with attribution. The package does not bundle the source distribution. Users stage it locally.
:::

:::

## Caveats

- Each standard group typically contains one or two numbered standards, so **numbered standards are sparse**. The framework's instructional density lives in the clarification-statement prose rather than in additional numbered standards. Analyses that look only at the strict count will undercount what the framework actually specifies for a teacher.
- Clarification statements are **teacher-facing pedagogical scaffolding, not enumerable sub-standards**. They're reachable through the separate `cybed:hasExample` predicate to keep this distinction visible at query time.

## Related

- [CSTA K-12 CS](csta-2017.qmd), the closest peer framework.
- [Cross-framework density spread](../queries/density-spread.qmd)
- [The vocabulary](../concepts/vocabulary.qmd), on why `cybed:Example`
  is separate from `cybed:Subpoint`.
